stem: A electronics testing study measures the battery life (hours) for a sample of 15 smartphone models. The sample mean is 342.8 hours with a sample standard deviation of 128.8 hours. However, 3 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 12 observations have a mean of 325.9 hours and standard deviation of 77.9 hours. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 342.8, 95% CI = [271.5, 414.1]. Without outliers: mean = 325.9, 95% CI = [280.9, 370.9]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [271.5, 414.1]. Point estimate: 334.4 hours.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [271.5, 414.1].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
